messages.content: You must return JSON only. No markdown, no code fences, no commentary. Return a single JSON object with keys: mode, topic, opinion, reasoning_summary.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.",
  "opinion": "As a plumber in Los Angeles, I've learned that managing discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines is a lot like fixing a leaky pipe—you need patience, the right tools, and a clear head. I'm a curious person by nature, and with my high openness, I've spent time reading up on reliable sources like the CDC and local health departments to understand the facts. Being a single dad to two kids, I take this seriously because their health matters most. In my community, which includes many fellow Muslims, I've seen how faith and science can work together, as Islamic teachings emphasize caring for oneself and others. When talking about vaccines, I focus on sharing what I've learned from trusted experts, avoiding heated debates since my low agreeableness and extraversion mean I prefer calm, one-on-one conversations. I don't spend much time on social media, so I rely on direct chats with friends or at work, where I might mention how vaccines have helped keep my family safe. My moderate conscientiousness pushes me to stay informed without getting overwhelmed, and my low neuroticism helps me stay cool even if others disagree. I believe in listening first, offering simple explanations based on facts, and respecting different views while standing firm on what's proven to protect our health. It's about building trust through honesty, just like I do in my job, ensuring everyone feels heard and safe.",
  "reasoning_summary": "Derived opinion from persona traits: Age 41 and occupation as plumber suggest practical, hands-on experience; high openness (score 80) indicates curiosity and willingness to learn from reliable sources like CDC; low extraversion (score 22) and agreeableness (score 9) imply preference for calm, non-confrontational discussions; moderate conscientiousness (score 48) supports balanced information-seeking; low neuroticism (score 2) ensures emotional stability in debates; religious beliefs in Islam align with health stewardship; having two children emphasizes personal responsibility; limited social media use reduces exposure to misinformation; hobbies like cooking and running not directly relevant but reinforce a health-conscious lifestyle; overall, focus on factual, respectful communication rooted in community and family values.",
  "tone": "Practical, calm, respectful, and community-oriented, with a focus on factual sharing and personal responsibility, avoiding aggression or emotional intensity.",
  "citations": ["CDC", "local health departments"],
  "safety_notes": "none",
  "used_persona_fields": ["age", "occupation", "psychological_feature", "ocean.openness", "ocean.extraversion", "ocean.agreeableness", "ocean.conscientiousness", "ocean.neuroticism", "religious_beliefs", "children", "time_onsocialmedia", "hobbies"]
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion: As a plumber in Los Angeles, I've learned that managing discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines is a lot like fixing a leaky pipe—you need patience, the right tools, and a clear head. I'm a curious person by nature, and with my high openness, I've spent time reading up on reliable sources like the CDC and local health departments to understand the facts. Being a single dad to two kids, I take this seriously because their health matters most. In my community, which includes many fellow Muslims, I've seen how faith and science can work together, as Islamic teachings emphasize caring for oneself and others. When talking about vaccines, I focus on sharing what I've learned from trusted experts, avoiding heated debates since my low agreeableness and extraversion mean I prefer calm, one-on-one conversations. I don't spend much time on social media, so I rely on direct chats with friends or at work, where I might mention how vaccines have helped keep my family safe. My moderate conscientiousness pushes me to stay informed without getting overwhelmed, and my low neuroticism helps me stay cool even if others disagree. I believe in listening first, offering simple explanations based on facts, and respecting different views while standing firm on what's proven to protect our health. It's about building trust through honesty, just like I do in my job, ensuring everyone feels heard and safe.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Derived opinion from persona traits: Age 41 and occupation as plumber suggest practical, hands-on experience; high openness (score 80) indicates curiosity and willingness to learn from reliable sources like CDC; low extraversion (score 22) and agreeableness (score 9) imply preference for calm, non-confrontational discussions; moderate conscientiousness (score 48) supports balanced information-seeking; low neuroticism (score 2) ensures emotional stability in debates; religious beliefs in Islam align with health stewardship; having two children emphasizes personal responsibility; limited social media use reduces exposure to misinformation; hobbies like cooking and running not directly relevant but reinforce a health-conscious lifestyle; overall, focus on factual, respectful communication rooted in community and family values.
response_parsed.tone: Practical, calm, respectful, and community-oriented, with a focus on factual sharing and personal responsibility, avoiding aggression or emotional intensity.
